---
title: "Serotype and antibiotic susceptibility of Group B Streptococcus in pregnant women in Santiago, Chile"
output:
  pdf_document:
    keep_tex: yes
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 6
    latex_engine: xelatex
header-includes:
   - \usepackage[default]{sourcesanspro}
   - \usepackage{amsmath}
   - \usepackage{float}
   - \usepackage{booktabs}
   - \usepackage{tabu}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, comment = NA, prompt = TRUE, tidy = T, tidy.opts=list(width.cutoff=60), echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, cache=T)

Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "C")

install_and_load_packages <- function(packages) {
  for (package in packages) {
    if (!require(package, character.only = TRUE)) {
      install.packages(package)
      library(package, character.only = TRUE)
    }
  }
}

necessary_packages <- c(
  "tidyverse",
  "readxl",
  "knitr",
  "kableExtra"
)

install_and_load_packages(necessary_packages)

```

\newpage

# Supplementary Material: Statistical Analysis

## S1. Data Preprocessing

Raw data were imported from datos.xlsx (n = 185 isolates). Susceptibility values for erythromycin and clindamycin contained numeric inhibition halo annotations (e.g., "R 10", "R 30", "R 62") that were recoded into binary variables: any value beginning with "R" was classified as resistant (R), and "S" as susceptible. Resistance phenotypes were grouped into five categories: cMLSB, iMLSB, L, M, and susceptible.

Final serotype distribution: Ia = 47, Ib = 26, II = 20, III = 41, IV = 13, V = 26, VI = 1, VII = 1, IX = 4, NT = 6 (total = 185).

```{r data-preprocessing, echo=FALSE}
datos <- read_excel("datos.xlsx")

datos <- datos %>%
  mutate(
    Ery  = if_else(str_starts(Eritromicina, "R"), "R", "S"),
    Cli  = if_else(str_starts(Clindamicina, "R"), "R", "S"),
    Pen  = Penicilina,
    Van  = Vancomicina,
    Phenotype = case_when(
      is.na(`Fenotipo Resistencia`)               ~ "Susceptible",
      str_starts(`Fenotipo Resistencia`, "MLSBc") ~ "cMLSB",
      str_starts(`Fenotipo Resistencia`, "MLSBi") ~ "iMLSB",
      str_starts(`Fenotipo Resistencia`, "M")     ~ "M",
      str_starts(`Fenotipo Resistencia`, "L")     ~ "L",
      TRUE                                        ~ "Other"
    ),
    Serotype = factor(Serotipo,
      levels = c("Ia","Ib","II","III","IV","V","VI","VII","IX","NT"))
  )

table(datos$Serotype)
table(datos$Ery)
table(datos$Cli)
table(datos$Phenotype)
```
\newpage

## S2. Overall Antimicrobial Susceptibility
Susceptibility proportions for each antibiotic were calculated over the total 185 isolates. Ninety-five percent confidence intervals were estimated using the Wilson score method (prop.test, without continuity correction).

```{r overall-susceptibility, echo=FALSE}
# Wilson 95% CI for a single proportion
wilson_ci <- function(x, n) {
  test <- prop.test(x, n, correct = FALSE)
  tibble(
    n_susceptible = x,
    n_total       = n,
    pct           = round(x / n * 100, 1),
    CI_low   = round(test$conf.int[1] * 100, 1),
    CI_high  = round(test$conf.int[2] * 100, 1)
  )
}

susc_global <- bind_rows(
  wilson_ci(sum(datos$Pen == "S"), 185) %>% mutate(Antibiotic = "Penicillin"),
  wilson_ci(sum(datos$Ery == "S"), 185) %>% mutate(Antibiotic = "Erythromycin"),
  wilson_ci(sum(datos$Cli == "S"), 185) %>% mutate(Antibiotic = "Clindamycin"),
  wilson_ci(sum(datos$Van == "S"), 185) %>% mutate(Antibiotic = "Vancomycin")
) %>%
  select(Antibiotic, n_susceptible, n_total, pct, CI_low, CI_high)

susc_global %>%
  rename(
    "Antibiotic"   = Antibiotic,
    "n (S)"        = n_susceptible,
    "n (total)"    = n_total,
    "% S"          = pct,
    "95% CI lower" = CI_low,
    "95% CI upper" = CI_high
  ) %>%
  kable(
    format    = "latex",
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    caption   = "Table S1. Overall antimicrobial susceptibility (n = 185)",
    align     = c("l","c","c","c","c","c")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("HOLD_position", "striped"),
    font_size     = 9,
    full_width    = FALSE
  )
```

\newpage

## S3. Antimicrobial Susceptibility by Serotype

Susceptible and resistant counts were calculated from raw data. Results were internally consistent across all serotypes. Ninety-five percent confidence intervals were estimated using the exact Clopperâ€“Pearson method.

```{r susceptibility-by-serotype, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
serotypes_analysis <- c("Ia","Ib","II","III","IV","V","IX","NT")

cp_ci <- function(x, n) {
  t <- binom.test(x, n)
  paste0("(", round(t$conf.int*100,1), "â€“", round(t$conf.int*100,1), ")")[1][2]
}

susc_by_serotype <- datos %>%
  filter(Serotipo %in% serotypes_analysis) %>%
  group_by(Serotipo) %>%
  summarise(
    n     = n(),
    Ery_S = sum(Ery == "S"),
    Cli_S = sum(Cli == "S"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Ery_pct = round(Ery_S / n * 100, 1),
    Cli_pct = round(Cli_S / n * 100, 1),
    Ery_CI  = map2_chr(Ery_S, n, cp_ci),
    Cli_CI  = map2_chr(Cli_S, n, cp_ci)
  )

susc_by_serotype %>%
  select(Serotipo, n, Ery_S, Ery_pct, Ery_CI, Cli_S, Cli_pct, Cli_CI) %>%
  rename(
    "Serotype"       = Serotipo,
    "n"              = n,
    "Ery (n S)"      = Ery_S,
    "Ery (%)"        = Ery_pct,
    "Ery 95% CI"     = Ery_CI,
    "Cli (n S)"      = Cli_S,
    "Cli (%)"        = Cli_pct,
    "Cli 95% CI"     = Cli_CI
  ) %>%
  kbl(
    format   = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption  = "Table S2. Antimicrobial susceptibility by serotype with 95\\% CI (Clopperâ€“Pearson)",
    align    = c("l","c","c","c","c","c","c","c"),
    linesep  = ""
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("HOLD_position", "striped"),
    font_size     = 9,
    full_width    = FALSE
  ) %>%
  add_header_above(
    c(" " = 2, "Erythromycin" = 3, "Clindamycin" = 3),
    bold = FALSE, line = TRUE
  ) %>%
  print()
```

\newpage

## S4. Resistance Phenotype Distribution

The cMLSB phenotype was the most prevalent (n = 44), distributed across 9 of the 10 identified serotypes. Phenotype counts per serotype were internally consistent with the manuscript values.

```{r phenotype-distribution, echo=FALSE}
tab_phenotype <- datos %>%
  filter(Phenotype != "Susceptible") %>%
  select(Serotipo, Phenotype) %>%
  table()

print(tab_phenotype)
fisher.test(tab_phenotype, simulate.p.value = TRUE, B = 10000)

datos %>%
  count(Phenotype) %>%
  mutate(
    pct     = round(n / 185 * 100, 1),
    CI_low  = map_dbl(n, ~prop.test(.x, 185, correct=FALSE)$conf.int[1]*100) %>% round(1),
    CI_high = map_dbl(n, ~prop.test(.x, 185, correct=FALSE)$conf.int[2]*100) %>% round(1),
    CI      = paste0("(", CI_low, "â€“", CI_high, "%)")
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  select(Phenotype, n, pct, CI) %>%
  rename("95% CI" = CI, "%" = pct) %>%
  kable(
    format   = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption  = "Table S3. Distribution of resistance phenotypes (n = 185)",
    align    = c("l","c","c","c")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("HOLD_position", "striped"),
    font_size     = 9,
    full_width    = FALSE
  )
```

\newpage

### Global Comparison of Susceptibility Across Serotypes

Because 31.25% of expected cell counts fell below 5 (serotypes IX and NT, n â‰¤ 4), Fisher's exact test with Monte Carlo simulation (10,000 replicates) was applied instead of chi-squared.

```{r global-fisher, echo=FALSE}
tab_ery <- datos %>%
  filter(Serotipo %in% serotypes_analysis) %>%
  mutate(Serotipo = droplevels(factor(Serotipo))) %>%
  select(Serotipo, Ery) %>%
  table()

tab_cli <- datos %>%
  filter(Serotipo %in% serotypes_analysis) %>%
  mutate(Serotipo = droplevels(factor(Serotipo))) %>%
  select(Serotipo, Cli) %>%
  table()

select_test <- function(tab, label) {
  chi      <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab))
  pct_low  <- mean(chi$expected < 5) * 100
  cat("--", label, "--\n")
  cat("Cells with expected count < 5:", round(pct_low, 2), "%\n")
  if (any(chi$expected < 5)) {
    f <- fisher.test(tab, simulate.p.value = TRUE, B = 10000)
    cat("Fisher's exact test (Monte Carlo), p =", round(f$p.value, 4), "\n\n")
    return(f)
  } else {
    cat("Chi-squared, X2 =", round(chi$statistic, 2),
        "df =", chi$parameter,
        "p =", round(chi$p.value, 4), "\n\n")
    return(chi)
  }
}

result_ery <- select_test(tab_ery, "Erythromycin")
result_cli <- select_test(tab_cli, "Clindamycin")
```
\newpage

## S6. Pairwise Comparisons Among Predominant Serotypes (Clindamycin)

Pairwise comparisons among the three most prevalent serotypes (Ia, III, V) were performed using Fisher's exact test with Bonferroni correction (adjusted Î± = 0.017 for three simultaneous comparisons).

```{r pairwise-serotypes, echo=FALSE}
pairwise_fisher <- function(df, s1, s2) {
  sub <- df %>% filter(Serotipo %in% c(s1, s2))
  tab <- table(sub$Serotipo, sub$Cli)
  f   <- fisher.test(tab)
  cat(s1, "vs", s2, "-- p =", round(f$p.value, 4), "\n")
  return(f)
}

pairwise_fisher(datos, "Ia",  "III")
pairwise_fisher(datos, "Ia",  "V")
pairwise_fisher(datos, "III", "V")
```

\newpage

## S7. Comparison with Historical Chilean Cohorts (Clindamycin)

Clindamycin susceptibility was compared against four previously published Chilean studies using two-proportion Z-tests (without continuity correction).

```{r historical-comparison, echo=FALSE}
compare_historical <- function(n_S_current, n_current, n_S_prev, n_prev, reference) {
  test <- prop.test(
    x         = c(n_S_current, n_S_prev),
    n         = c(n_current,   n_prev),
    correct   = FALSE,
    alternative = "two.sided"
  )
  tibble(
    Reference       = reference,
    pct_current     = round(n_S_current / n_current * 100, 1),
    pct_historical  = round(n_S_prev    / n_prev    * 100, 1),
    p_value         = if_else(test$p.value < 0.0001, "< 0.0001",
                              as.character(round(test$p.value, 4))),
    significant     = if_else(test$p.value < 0.05, "Yes *", "No")
  )
}

n_cli_S <- sum(datos$Cli == "S")

bind_rows(
  compare_historical(n_cli_S, 185, round(0.967*183), 183, "Belmar et al. 2002"),
  compare_historical(n_cli_S, 185, round(0.960*65),   65, "MartĂ­nez et al. 2004"),
  compare_historical(n_cli_S, 185, round(0.869*99),   99, "AbarzĂşa et al. 2011"),
  compare_historical(n_cli_S, 185, round(0.863*167), 167, "AbarzĂşa et al. 2014")
) %>%
  rename(
    "Reference"        = Reference,
    "% This study"     = pct_current,
    "% Historical"     = pct_historical,
    "p-value"          = p_value,
    "Significant"      = significant
  ) %>%
  kable(
    format   = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption  = "Table S4. Clindamycin susceptibility: this study vs. prior Chilean cohorts",
    align    = c("l","c","c","c","c")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("HOLD_position", "striped"),
    font_size     = 9,
    full_width    = FALSE
  )
```

\newpage

## S8. Phenotype Comparison: Serotypes Ia vs. III

```{r Ia-vs-III-phenotypes, echo=FALSE}
tab_Ia_III <- datos %>%
  filter(Serotipo %in% c("Ia","III"), Phenotype != "Susceptible") %>%
  mutate(Serotipo = droplevels(factor(Serotipo))) %>%
  select(Serotipo, Phenotype) %>%
  table()

print(tab_Ia_III)
fisher.test(tab_Ia_III, simulate.p.value = TRUE, B = 10000)

n_Ia  <- sum(datos$Serotipo == "Ia")
n_III <- sum(datos$Serotipo == "III")

# cMLSB: Ia (10/47) vs III (14/41)
prop.test(
  c(sum(datos$Serotipo == "Ia"  & datos$Phenotype == "cMLSB"),
    sum(datos$Serotipo == "III" & datos$Phenotype == "cMLSB")),
  c(n_Ia, n_III),
  correct = FALSE
)

# L phenotype: Ia (5/47) vs III (1/41)
l_Ia  <- sum(datos$Serotipo == "Ia"  & datos$Phenotype == "L")
l_III <- sum(datos$Serotipo == "III" & datos$Phenotype == "L")

fisher.test(matrix(c(l_Ia, n_Ia - l_Ia, l_III, n_III - l_III), nrow = 2))
```

\newpage

## S9. Serotype Distribution: Comparison with MartĂ­nez et al. (2004)

Serotype distribution was compared with the only prior Chilean study 
reporting serotype-level data (MartĂ­nez et al. 2004, n = 65) using 
Fisher's exact test with Monte Carlo simulation and pairwise 
two-proportion Z-tests. Serotypes absent in MartĂ­nez et al. 2004 
(IV, VI, VII, IX) were excluded from pairwise comparisons.

```{r serotype-temporal-comparison, echo=FALSE}
shared_serotypes <- c("Ia","Ib","II","III","V","NT")

martinez_2004 <- tibble(
  Serotype   = shared_serotypes,
  n_martinez = c(20, 3, 14, 24, 1, 3)
)

this_study <- tibble(
  Serotype = shared_serotypes,
  n_current = c(47, 26, 21, 41, 26, 5)
)

tab_temporal <- rbind(
  this_study$n_current,
  martinez_2004$n_martinez
)
colnames(tab_temporal) <- shared_serotypes
rownames(tab_temporal) <- c("This study (2023)", "MartĂ­nez et al. 2004")

print(tab_temporal)

# Global Fisher test
fisher.test(tab_temporal, simulate.p.value = TRUE, B = 10000)

# Pairwise comparisons per serotype
compare_serotype_temporal <- function(sero) {
  n1    <- this_study$n_current[this_study$Serotype == sero]
  n2    <- martinez_2004$n_martinez[martinez_2004$Serotype == sero]
  test  <- prop.test(c(n1, n2), c(185, 65), correct = FALSE)
  tibble(
    Serotype          = sero,
    pct_current       = round(n1 / 185 * 100, 1),
    pct_martinez      = round(n2 / 65  * 100, 1),
    p_value           = if_else(test$p.value < 0.0001, "< 0.0001",
                                as.character(round(test$p.value, 4))),
    significant       = if_else(test$p.value < 0.05, "Yes *", "No")
  )
}

bind_rows(lapply(shared_serotypes, compare_serotype_temporal)) %>%
  rename(
    "Serotype"               = Serotype,
    "% This study"           = pct_current,
    "% MartĂ­nez et al. 2004" = pct_martinez,
    "p-value"                = p_value,
    "Significant"            = significant
  ) %>%
  kable(
    format   = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption  = "Table S5. Serotype distribution: this study vs. MartĂ­nez et al. 2004",
    align    = c("l","c","c","c","c")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("HOLD_position", "striped"),
    font_size     = 9,
    full_width    = FALSE
  )
```
